slides.title: Motivation and Use Cases for CNNs
slides.content:
Why are CNNs crucial in the field of AI?
- CNNs excel at image processing tasks.
- They are pivotal in computer vision applications such as object detection, facial recognition, and self-driving cars.
- Use cases extend to video analysis, healthcare, and more.

slides.title: Convolutional Operations: A Deep Dive
slides.content:
- Convolutions help extract spatial features from images.
- Imagine sliding a small window over an image and calculating dot products to uncover features.

slides.title: Kernels (Filters) and Strides
slides.content:
- Kernels capture specific features like edges.
- Strides determine the movement of the kernel across the image.

slides.title: Feature Maps, Pooling, and Activation
slides.content:
What follows convolution?
- Feature maps show extracted features.
- Pooling reduces spatial dimensions (max pooling as a common example).
- Activation functions like ReLU introduce non-linearity.

slides.title: Typical CNN Architecture & Training Overview
slides.content:
- Typical blocks: Convolutional layers, pooling layers, and fully connected layers.
- Training involves loss calculation and optimization (e.g., using SGD or Adam).

slides.title: Tackling Overfitting: Regularization Techniques
slides.content:
- Overfitting can plague model accuracy on unseen data.
- Regularization methods: Dropout layers and data augmentation are essential to mitigate this.

slides.title: Code Walkthrough & Summary
slides.content:
- Let's explore a simple CNN code snippet.
- Summing up: CNNs are fundamental in processing visual data, leveraging layers to extract intricate features.

visual_assets.code:
model = Sequential()
model.add(Conv2D(32, kernel_size=(3, 3), activation='relu', input_shape=input_shape))
visual_assets.caption: Code for a simple CNN
